title: leptosiphon pygmaeus subsp. pygmaeus
intro: Leptosiphon pygmaeus subsp. pygmaeus, also known as the pygmy blue eyed Mary, is a flowering plant species in the phlox family, native to the western United States. This plant is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Leptosiphon pygmaeus subsp. pygmaeus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and boost the immune system. Leptosiphon pygmaeus subsp. pygmaeus is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for its medicinal uses, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
